page.title: Marbella Jet Ski and Snorkel Combo: Adrenaline Meets Underwater Exploration

data.meta_description: Experience a Marbella jet ski and snorkel combo: ride the Costa del Sol then explore underwater. Includes gear, skipper, drinks. Book your 4h adventure today.
data.summary: This page covers the Marbella jet ski and snorkel combo experience, combining high-speed jet ski riding with snorkelling in crystal-clear coves. It details what to expect, which boats offer jet skis, pricing, and how to book.
key_facts: Combo experience: jet ski riding + snorkelling in Marbella's coves
Available on Mangusta 80 (white & grey) – jet ski included free
Minimum charter 4 hours from Puerto Banús
Includes licensed skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance, VAT
Snorkel gear provided on board (mask, fins, snorkel)
Explore Cala del Faro, Cala Cortés, or Cabopino coves
Jet ski session supervised by your skipper for safety
Price from €4,719 for up to 12 guests (jet ski + boat)

faq.q: What is included in the Marbella jet ski and snorkel combo?
faq.a: The combo includes a 4-hour charter on a Mangusta 80 (24m) with a free jet ski, plus full snorkel gear (mask, fins, snorkel). Also included: a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, cava, white wine, light snacks, insurance, and Spanish VAT.

faq.q: Which boats offer the jet ski and snorkel combo?
faq.a: The Mangusta 80 (white and grey variants) both include a jet ski free of charge with a minimum 4-hour charter. Other boats in our fleet can be arranged with a jet ski addition – enquire via WhatsApp for pricing.

faq.q: How long is the jet ski and snorkel combo experience?
faq.a: The minimum charter is 4 hours, which gives you ample time for a jet ski session (typically 30–45 minutes) plus snorkelling stops at one or two coves. You can extend to 6 or 8 hours for a more relaxed pace.

faq.q: Where do we go snorkelling during the combo?
faq.a: Popular snorkelling spots include Cala del Faro, Cala Cortés, and the coves near Cabopino. Your skipper will choose the best location based on sea conditions and visibility, typically within 15–30 minutes from Puerto Banús.

faq.q: Do I need a licence to ride the jet ski?
faq.a: Yes, in Spain you need a valid jet ski licence (PNB or similar) to ride independently. If you don't have one, your skipper can give you a supervised ride as a passenger. Please confirm your licence status when booking.

faq.q: Is snorkel gear provided, or do I need to bring my own?
faq.a: We provide masks, fins, and snorkels on board – all cleaned and sanitised. If you have your own gear, you're welcome to bring it. We recommend bringing a towel, sunscreen, and a change of clothes.

faq.q: Can we customise the combo (e.g., add more jet ski time)?
faq.a: Absolutely. You can add extra jet ski time or request a specific itinerary. Contact us via WhatsApp before booking to arrange customisations. Prices for additional jet ski time are quoted on request.

faq.q: How do I book the Marbella jet ski and snorkel combo?
faq.a: Book directly on this page by clicking the 'Enquire now' button, or message us on WhatsApp. We'll confirm availability, send a secure payment link, and provide all pre-departure details. A 50% deposit secures your date.

data.body_html:
Want to feel the spray of the Costa del Sol on a jet ski, then float face-down over a rocky reef teeming with fish? The Marbella jet ski and snorkel combo gives you both – without the faff of renting separate kit or dealing with multiple operators. You get a luxury motor yacht as your base, a jet ski to rip around on, and snorkel gear to explore the clear Mediterranean waters. All in one seamless half-day charter from Puerto Banús.

What You Get: Boat + Jet Ski + Snorkel Gear

This isn't a generic package – it's a proper boat charter with a jet ski thrown in. The star of the show is the Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 guests), available in white or grey, both of which include a jet ski free of charge with a minimum 4-hour booking. You get the full boat experience: a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, cava, white wine, light snacks, insurance, and Spanish VAT (21%). Snorkel masks, fins, and snorkels are provided on board – just bring a towel and sunscreen.

How the Combo Works

You depart from Puerto Banús and cruise along the coast while your skipper briefs you on safety and the day's plan. Once you reach a sheltered cove – typically Cala del Faro, Cala Cortés, or the coves near Cabopino – you take turns on the jet ski. The skipper supervises the session, so even if you're a first-timer, you can ride as a passenger. After the adrenaline, you drop anchor and snorkel the rocky bottoms and seagrass meadows where bream, wrasse, and sometimes octopus hang out.

Pricing: What You Pay

The Mangusta 80 combo starts at €4,719 for a minimum 4-hour charter. That includes the jet ski at no extra cost. If you want a longer day (6 or 8 hours), the price adjusts accordingly – enquire via WhatsApp for exact rates. Other boats in our fleet (like the Azimut 58 Flybridge or Ferretti 94) can be arranged with a jet ski addition, but pricing is on request.

BoatLengthGuestsMin. CharterPrice from
Mangusta 80 (White or Grey)24m124 hours€4,719

Best Snorkelling Spots Near Marbella

The Costa del Sol has surprisingly good snorkelling when you know where to go. Our skippers favour these spots:

  • Cala del Faro – a rocky cove west of Marbella with clear water and good fish life.
  • Cala Cortés – a small sheltered bay near Estepona, popular for its calm conditions.
  • Cabopino – the dunes and rocky reefs here attract a variety of species.
  • Río Verde – a quieter spot east of Puerto Banús, often less crowded.

Your skipper will pick the best option based on wind, swell, and visibility – all within 15–30 minutes of the marina.

What to Bring and Wear

We provide the snorkel gear and towels on board, but you'll want: sunscreen (reef-safe if possible), a hat, sunglasses with a strap, a change of clothes, and a light jacket for the ride back. If you have your own jet ski licence, bring it – otherwise, you can ride as a passenger under the skipper's supervision. The water temperature in summer hits 24–26°C, so a thin wetsuit isn't essential but can help if you get cold easily.

Who Is This Combo For?

Groups of friends, families with older kids (minimum age for jet ski passenger is usually 12), or couples who want a mix of thrill and chill. The Mangusta 80 has plenty of deck space to sunbathe, a saloon with air conditioning, and a forward sun pad – so non-riders can relax while others take the jet ski. It works well for birthday celebrations, bachelor/bachelorette parties, or just a memorable day out.

How to Book Your Marbella Jet Ski and Snorkel Combo

Ready to go? Browse our full fleet or contact us directly via WhatsApp. We'll check availability for your preferred date, send you a secure payment link (50% deposit to secure), and provide all pre-departure details – meeting point at Puerto Banús, what to expect, and your skipper's contact. Book at least 48 hours in advance for best availability, especially in July and August.

Frequently Asked Questions

What is included in the Marbella jet ski and snorkel combo?

The combo includes a 4-hour charter on a Mangusta 80 (24m) with a free jet ski, plus full snorkel gear (mask, fins, snorkel). Also included: a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, cava, white wine, light snacks, insurance, and Spanish VAT.

Which boats offer the jet ski and snorkel combo?

The Mangusta 80 (white and grey variants) both include a jet ski free of charge with a minimum 4-hour charter. Other boats in our fleet can be arranged with a jet ski addition – enquire via WhatsApp for pricing.

How long is the jet ski and snorkel combo experience?

The minimum charter is 4 hours, which gives you ample time for a jet ski session (typically 30–45 minutes) plus snorkelling stops at one or two coves. You can extend to 6 or 8 hours for a more relaxed pace.

Where do we go snorkelling during the combo?

Popular snorkelling spots include Cala del Faro, Cala Cortés, and the coves near Cabopino. Your skipper will choose the best location based on sea conditions and visibility, typically within 15–30 minutes from Puerto Banús.

Do I need a licence to ride the jet ski?

Yes, in Spain you need a valid jet ski licence (PNB or similar) to ride independently. If you don't have one, your skipper can give you a supervised ride as a passenger. Please confirm your licence status when booking.

Is snorkel gear provided, or do I need to bring my own?

We provide masks, fins, and snorkels on board – all cleaned and sanitised. If you have your own gear, you're welcome to bring it. We recommend bringing a towel, sunscreen, and a change of clothes.

Can we customise the combo (e.g., add more jet ski time)?

Absolutely. You can add extra jet ski time or request a specific itinerary. Contact us via WhatsApp before booking to arrange customisations. Prices for additional jet ski time are quoted on request.

How do I book the Marbella jet ski and snorkel combo?

Book directly on this page by clicking the 'Enquire now' button, or message us on WhatsApp. We'll confirm availability, send a secure payment link, and provide all pre-departure details. A 50% deposit secures your date.
